reasons: Tigreal can group or knock up enemies, giving Aurora a clear freeze and burst window.
Aurora can layer control after Tigreal starts the fight to extend the enemy's disabled state.

proof.summary: Tigreal starts the crowd-control sequence, while Aurora layers freeze and burst onto the controlled enemies.
worksBestWhen: Aurora waits for Tigreal's real engage.
The enemy team is grouped in a choke point.
failureCases: Tigreal engages before Aurora is in range.
Aurora uses freeze before the main engage.
The enemy spreads before Tigreal reaches them.

reasons: Atlas can pull enemies together, allowing Aurora to freeze or burst multiple targets at once.
Aurora can punish the grouped pull area with reliable magic control.

proof.summary: Atlas creates the grouped target area, and Aurora converts it into freeze control and magic burst.
worksBestWhen: Aurora saves her key skill for the pull finish.
failureCases: Atlas misses or catches only the tank.
Aurora is too far to follow.
Enemies cleanse or disengage after the pull.

reasons: Johnson can deliver Aurora into a surprise engage angle where her freeze and burst are easier to apply.
Aurora can follow the crash with control before enemies fully reposition.

proof.summary: Johnson creates the sudden arrival and crash disruption, while Aurora adds freeze and burst follow-up.
worksBestWhen: Aurora rides with Johnson or is already waiting near the crash path.
failureCases: The enemy has vision of the car path.

reasons: Franco can isolate a target with hook or suppression, letting Aurora land controlled burst without starting the fight herself.
Aurora can freeze and burst the target after Franco brings them into range.

proof.summary: Franco catches one target, and Aurora follows with freeze and burst to secure the pick.
worksBestWhen: Aurora holds skills until the target is displaced.

reasons: Kaja's suppression can hold a target long enough for Aurora to add freeze and burst damage.
Aurora can follow Kaja's pull with reliable single-target magic burst.

proof.summary: Kaja pulls and suppresses the target, while Aurora follows with burst control to finish the pick.
worksBestWhen: Kaja targets a squishy or mobile hero.
Aurora positions close enough to follow immediately.
failureCases: Aurora uses cooldowns before the pull.
The enemy punishes Aurora's short follow-up range.
